title: Columbaria suaveolens
intro: Columbaria suaveolens, also known as the sweet pagoda tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Rhamnaceae, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The health benefits of Columbaria suaveolens include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, such as fever, coughs, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Columbaria suaveolens include tea, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Columbaria suaveolens is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Columbaria, which consists of several species of flowering plants.
